page: Amazon Web Services c6i.12xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the AWS Nitro System. It features 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores based on the x86_64 Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at a clock speed of 3.5 GHz. The instance is equipped with 96.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage and offering a baseline network bandwidth of 19 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong capabilities in compute-intensive operations. The instance achieves a PassMark CPU score of 47,897.3, placing it in the top 25% tier, with notable strength in CPU encryption tests scoring 42,481.53. In image processing workloads, the Geekbench score of 15,272.0 also ranks in the top 25% category. Standard multi-core operations, such as stress-ng multi-core CPU performance (46,465.78) and Geekbench multi-core (15,737.0), fall within the average range for its hardware class. Memory bandwidth tests show a read-only large block speed of 165.37 GB/sec and a write speed of 68.06 GB/sec.

The underlying Intel Xeon processor supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 (specifically avx512f and avx512_vnni) for vector and deep learning acceleration, alongside hardware-assisted cryptography via AES and SHA_NI. These features, combined with the dedicated CPU allocation, make the instance highly suitable for compute-bound workloads such as batch processing, distributed analytics, high-performance web serving, and CPU-based machine learning inference. The 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio offers a cost-efficient profile for workloads that demand high raw processing power without requiring massive memory footprints.
description: Amazon Web Services c6i.12xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance powered by the AWS Nitro System and an Intel Xeon 8375C processor. It features 48 dedicated vCPUs (24 physical cores) running at 3.5 GHz, paired with 96.0 GB of DDR4 memory, yielding a 2.0 GB per core ratio. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs but provides a baseline network bandwidth of 19 Gbps. Benchmark data highlights strong performance in CPU encryption and image processing, placing it in the top 25% tier, while multi-core processing and memory bandwidth remain in the average range. Supported instruction sets like AVX-512 and SHA_NI enhance its efficiency for cryptographic and vector calculations. This hardware profile is optimized for compute-heavy workloads such as batch processing, high-performance web serving, and distributed analytics, offering a resource-dense configuration for processing-intensive tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c6i.12xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 48 vCPUs, 96 GB DDR4 RAM, and a 3.5 GHz Intel Xeon processor, delivering strong performance for compute-heavy workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c6i.12xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance with 48 vCPUs, 96 GB DDR4 RAM, and 19 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
tagline: A high-density compute instance featuring forty-eight dedicated vCPUs and ninety-six gigabytes of memory for processing-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 48 dedicated vCPUs and 24 physical cores via Intel Xeon 8375C processors.
Equipped with 96.0 GB of DDR4 memory at a 2.0 GB per vCPU ratio.
Delivers 19 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with Nitro hypervisor integration.
Supports AVX-512 and SHA_NI instruction sets for accelerated vector and cryptographic tasks.
Strong benchmark performance in CPU encryption and image processing workloads.
Optimized for batch processing, distributed analytics, and high-performance web serving.
